title: Operation
description: The canonical apigen operation descriptor (SPEC §4). The neutral contract every extractor emits and every plugin consumes. One Operation = one selected export (action, live query, or class member per §10).

id.description: Canonical fully-qualified slug and cross-plugin reference key, derived purely from namespace/path (e.g. 'transform/humanize/humanize-bytes'). Globally unique within a merged descriptor; never re-cased. Deterministic (derivation-stable) but NOT refactor-stable: moving/renaming a file or export re-mints it. Refactor-stability is a non-goal; never papered over with a source @id annotation (Tenet 1).

host.description: Owning language runtime / host that produced and can serve this operation (e.g. 'ts', 'py', 'rust'). Polyglot descriptors mix hosts.

namespace.description: Package segment, sourced from --namespace or the tsconfig folder (§4/§5). Casing-neutral.

path.description: Hierarchical identity path: file → export… (§4/§5). Named export -> [file, name]; single default fn -> [file]; default object -> [file, 'default', ...keys] recursing nested props; index.* drops its file segment.

kind.description: Classification of the underlying export. 'action' = callable export; 'query' = serializable-data const served live (carries type, not value); 'constructor'/'instance-method' per §10. Non-serializable, non-callable exports are skipped + warned (never emitted).

async.description: True if the export is async (returns a Promise).

streaming.description: True if the export returns an AsyncIterable/Generator/Stream (streaming implemented in v2, §11). 'output' describes the per-chunk element type, unwrapped.

safe.description: Idempotent / no-side-effects hint (§4/§5). Defaults from kind (query->true, action->false); overridable at projection time via config, never a source annotation (Tenet 1). Drives HTTP verb + cacheability (safe->GET, unsafe->POST) and gRPC idempotency-level.

input.description: Params object as JSON Schema 2020-12. The ctx first param is excluded (name match only) and the middleware data-wrapper is dissolved — this is the bare domain params object.

output.description: Return type as JSON Schema 2020-12 with Promise<T>/stream wrappers unwrapped to T (per-chunk element type when streaming).

envelope.description: Effective request-side envelope as JSON Schema 2020-12 — the middleware-contributed side-channel for this operation, merged with overrides applied. Empty when no middleware contributes.

typeText.description: Optional language-tagged textual type rendering (same-host sugar). null when unavailable; non-host targets ignore it.

Segment.title: Segment
Segment.description: A casing-neutral name segment (§4/§5). Identity is the tokenized 'words'; 'raw' preserves the original spelling. Transports derive their own casing from 'words' via @adhd/apigen-naming.

raw.description: Original spelling as it appeared in source, e.g. 'humanizeBytes'.

TypeText.title: TypeText
TypeText.description: Language-tagged textual rendering of a type — optional same-host sugar (§4).

lang.description: Language tag for input/output text, e.g. 'ts'.

input.description: Textual rendering of the input/params type.

output.description: Textual rendering of the output/return type (unwrapped).

JSONSchema.title: JSONSchema
JSONSchema.description: A JSON Schema 2020-12 document fragment — the apigen type IR (§4/§16). No separate IR: named types, discriminated unions / enum-with-data / Result/Option (oneOf + const tag + $ref), nominal/branded types (named $def), and recursion ($ref) are represented faithfully. Wire convention: 64-bit ints/decimals are string-encoded ({ type: 'string', format: 'int64' }). Optional extractor-derived x-apigen-* hints + a fidelity flag are advisory codegen aids only, never required for correctness, never a source annotation. Modeled as an open object because a JSON Schema is structurally an arbitrary keyword object.

$ref.description: Reference to a definition, e.g. '#/$defs/User' (enables recursion).

x-apigen-nominal.description: Extractor-derived hint: this $def originated from a nominal/branded type. Validation does NOT enforce nominality (on the wire it is the base type); codegen may re-introduce the brand.

x-apigen-enum-repr.description: Extractor-derived hint: how an enum-like type should be represented in idiomatic codegen (e.g. 'enum' | 'union'). Advisory.

x-apigen-logical.description: Optional logical-type kind hint (@adhd/apigen-logical §4.1). Advisory dispatch accelerator only; structure (format/$ref/oneOf) is authoritative. Invariant [inv:hints-advisory]: correctness never depends on this key being present.

x-apigen-codec.description: Optional stable LogicalTypeId of the codec owning this node (@adhd/apigen-logical §4.1). Scalars: the JSON-Schema format; nominal/union: a namespace-qualified id (e.g. 'cli.User'). Advisory only.

x-apigen-ctor.description: Optional decode hint (@adhd/apigen-logical §4.1): the static/instance constructor used to rebuild a nominal instance from the decoded field bag (e.g. 'fromJSON'). Absent => schema-projected construction. Advisory only.

x-apigen-tojson.description: Optional encode hint (@adhd/apigen-logical §4.1): the method projecting a nominal instance to its field bag (e.g. 'toJSON'). Absent => structural field projection. Advisory only.

logicalTypeVersion.description: Optional version stamp of the logical-type wire table / extension vocabulary this fragment was produced against (@adhd/apigen-logical LOGICAL_TYPE_VERSION). Bumped on any wire-table or pinned-lib-version change; advisory.

fidelity.description: Optional fidelity of this schema fragment vs the source type. 'full' = lossless (absence implies full); 'lossy' = source could not be fully represented (the only true residual is generic factoring). Codegen may warn on 'lossy'.
